name: Deep Dish Pizza
recipeIngredient: 1 package Health Choice brand shredded pizza cheese
A generous handful of fresh basil leaves, chopped (dried is okay if that's all you have)
4 or 5 large cloves of fresh garlic, minced
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, mix yeast, salt, sugar, 1 cup flour, and warm water until well combined.
2. Add remaining flour to form soft dough. Knead for a few minutes, cover, and let rise.
3. While the dough rises, sauté onions in red wine until caramelized. Drain thawed spinach and break up tomatoes slightly. Set tomatoes aside to drain.
4. When dough has doubled in size (usually less than an hour), punch it down. Spray a 10-inch spring form pan with cooking spray.
5. Press dough into the pan, stretching up the sides. Spread shredded cheese over the crust.
6. Top with tomato mixture mixed with basil leaves. Season with salt, oregano, and black pepper. Sprinkle minced garlic over tomatoes.
7. Add sautéed onions on top of garlic. Layer spinach and sliced tomato decoratively on top.
8. Bake at 425°F in the middle of the oven for about 45 minutes or until filling is heated through.
Note: Use any fillings you like, such as artichokes, mushrooms, eggplant, roasted peppers, and broccoli. The Healthy Choice fake cheese melted well, as did the Kraft fat-free singles (sharp cheddar flavor).
